name: KonaTel, Inc.
business: KonaTel, Inc. provides Hosted Services through Apeiron Systems, including a suite of CPaaS services such as VoIP, SMS/MMS, and IoT data management, and Mobile Services through IM Telecom, including retail/wholesale cellular voice/text/data and government-subsidized Lifeline and ACP services. It sells to individual and business customers nationwide, as well as low-income households eligible for Lifeline, through websites, sales staff, retail locations, and independent sales organizations.

exposures.sentence: Our principal products and services, provided through Apeiron Systems and IM Telecom, include our CPaaS suite of services ( SIP/VoIP, SMS/MMS, POTS Replacement ), wholesale and retail mobile voice and mobile data IoT services, wholesale voice termination services and our ETC and ACP subsidized services for low-income Americans.
Additionally, Apeiron s Cloud Services include Information Data Dips, SD-WAN and IoT data and device management, of which IoT provides device connectivity via wireless 4G/5G.

catalysts.sentence: We continue to remain optimistic that the IM Telecom health care vertical will begin to accelerate in 2026.
The first 10,000 SIM cards have been purchased by and delivered to Viva for deployment. The Company has completed its pre-launch obligations; we continue to wait for launch implementation by Viva.

catalysts.event: POTS replacement solution targeting large telecom providers
catalysts.sentence: one of the Company s new services, deployed and tested throughout 2025, includes a wireless POTS replacement solution targeted at large national telecommunication service providers.

tamClaims.claim: 22 million commercial copper-wire POTS analog phone lines scheduled for phase-out
tamClaims.sentence: Based on FCC data, it is estimated there are approximately 22 million commercial (i.e., used in commercial operations) copper-wire Plain Old Telephone Service ( POTS ) analog phone lines.

tamClaims.claim: 7 million current Lifeline users and 34 million eligible Lifeline customers
tamClaims.sentence: Considering there are approximately 7,000,000 current Lifeline users and approximately 34,000,000 eligible Lifeline customers (according to Universal Service Administrative Company, a governmental body that administers the Universal Service Fund of the FCC [the USF ]), this would be a draconian move; however, it is a possibility.
